title: Ephesians: Walk Worthy
objective: Practice gospel-shaped relationships and stand firm with the armor of God.

required_passages.text: Walk in a manner worthy... put on the new self... be imitators of God... submit to one another... put on the whole armor of God... stand firm.

segments.audio_script: Paul turns from theology to practice: how redeemed people walk together and resist evil.

Listen to Ephesians 4-6. Paul urges humility, patience, and unity in the Spirit. He calls believers to put off the old self and put on the new. He applies gospel ethics to speech, sexuality, work, family, and mutual submission. He concludes with the armor of God and persistent prayer in spiritual warfare.
Household and social structures are transformed not by domination but by Christlike love and service.
Sanctification is communal and practical. Spiritual warfare is resisted through truth, righteousness, faith, Scripture, and prayer—not worldly methods.
Key themes include worthy walk, new self, holy relationships, unity, spiritual warfare, and persevering prayer.

Review question: What is the armor of God that Paul describes in Ephesians 6? Answer: the belt of truth, breastplate of righteousness, feet fitted with the gospel of peace, shield of faith, helmet of salvation, and sword of the Spirit which is the word of God.
segments.correct_answer: belt of truth, breastplate of righteousness, gospel of peace, shield of faith, helmet of salvation, and sword of the Spirit
acceptable_variants: the six pieces of armor in ephesians 6 — truth righteousness gospel faith salvation and the word
the full armor of god: truth belt, righteousness breastplate, peace shoes, faith shield, salvation helmet, word sword
ephesians 6:14-17 — the complete armor for standing against spiritual forces

segments.audio_script: The gospel reshapes life and equips us to stand. Next, Philippians teaches joy and humility in Christ.

vocabulary.definition: Paul's metaphor in Ephesians 6:10-18 for the spiritual resources available to believers in the cosmic conflict against evil; the imagery draws on Isaiah 59 where God himself wears armor
Paul's terms in Ephesians 6:12 for the spiritual forces behind human opposition; the Christian's struggle is not merely human but involves cosmic evil requiring divine armor
